national_community_engagement: Broadcast of the Provincial Military Governor's message on compliance with EVD control measures via various channels (radio, schools, social media); advocacy meetings with the Provincial Government Spokesperson, the Governorate's head of communication and the State Protocol Service chief; 5 advocacy meetings (Provincial EPST division head Orientale 1, Youth division head, provincial Gender/Family/Child division head, FARDC commander in Mangala HZ, Ituri provincial committee of traditional healers) — to prevent pupils being expelled from schools for lack of masks, mobilise youth organisations and women's associations in community engagement; promotion of the local toll-free number (0821419595).

Rumours identified: HGR Bunia accused of spreading Ebola in community toilets; allegations of lethal injections at ETC; questions about quality of care, treatment costs if result is negative, and conjugal cohabitation. A rumour response plan with targeted messages must be activated.

Rumours identified: (1) Traditional healers claim to have plant-based remedies to cure EVD. (2) Previous interventions had no positive impact — questioning NGOs (AIDES, TEARFUND, OXFAM, UNICEF). (3) Threat to burn LENGABO hospital if a tarpaulin is installed. (4) Demand for mandatory prior consultation of village and groupement chiefs before any activity, with a joint committee including community representatives. (5) Fear that polio vaccination will be seen as injecting the Ebola virus into children. — A rumour response plan with targeted messages must be activated.

Rumours identified: (1) Persistence of belief in plant-based traditional treatments. (2) Distrust of vaccines — particularly concern that polio vaccination will be seen as injecting the Ebola virus into children. — Targeted rumour response plan must be activated.

In the North Kivu 19,832 people sensitised; 3,221 households visited through the involvement of 658 community health workers (RECO); 813 community leaders committed; 46 community alerts reported and 1 rumour clarified; 18 local radios engaged with 3 formats produced and broadcast. Mass sensitisation of 405 final-year students at the ENAFEP centre in Kabunga on EVD prevention measures and signs. Advocacy meeting in Kahungu village (EVD epicentre) with 40 youth leaders, women leaders, school representatives and religious leaders on their involvement in the fight against EVD. 6,961 people sensitised and 1,567 households visited
